articles.headline: Government Unveils Ambitious National Digital Public Infrastructure Policy
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today announced a comprehensive National Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Policy, aimed at leveraging technology for inclusive growth and efficient service delivery.
• The policy focuses on building interoperable digital platforms for identity, payments, data exchange, and governance, inspired by the success of Aadhaar and UPI.
• Key objectives include enhancing digital literacy, fostering innovation in the tech ecosystem, and ensuring data privacy and security for all citizens.
• It seeks to create a robust framework for public-private partnerships in developing and deploying DPI solutions across various sectors.
• The policy is expected to streamline access to government services, boost digital economy, and bridge the digital divide, especially in rural areas.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Role of IT in governance
prelimsPoints: National Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Policy aims for inclusive growth and service delivery.
Focus areas: identity, payments, data exchange, governance platforms.
Inspired by Aadhaar and UPI models.
Key objectives: digital literacy, innovation, data privacy, security.
Promotes public-private partnerships in tech development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of DPI to transform governance and public service delivery in India, citing examples like Aadhaar and UPI.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a national DPI policy, including data security, privacy concerns, and digital exclusion.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships in building and scaling digital infrastructure.
Examine how DPI can contribute to India's economic growth and social equity.
Critically assess the 'Digital India' vision in light of this new policy.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration depicting interconnected digital nodes and services, symbolizing Digital Public Infrastructure.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processes more transactions than many global payment systems combined, showcasing the power of effective DPI.

articles.headline: Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram, Nuclear Scientist and Architect of India's Atomic Program, Passes Away at 88
articles.explainer:
• Renowned Indian nuclear scientist Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram passed away today at the age of 88, leaving behind a profound legacy in India's scientific landscape.
• Dr. Chidambaram was a pivotal figure in India's nuclear program, serving as the Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission (AEC) and Principal Scientific Adviser to the Government of India.
• He played a crucial role in the Pokhran-II nuclear tests in 1998, providing critical scientific leadership and technical expertise.
• His contributions extended to materials science, high-pressure physics, and development of advanced nuclear reactors.
• Dr. Chidambaram was a recipient of the Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian honor, recognizing his immense services to the nation.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
prelimsPoints: Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram (1936-2025) was a leading Indian nuclear scientist.
Served as Chairman of Atomic Energy Commission and Principal Scientific Adviser.
Instrumental in the Pokhran-II nuclear tests (1998).
Awarded Padma Vibhushan.
Contributions in materials science and high-pressure physics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of India's nuclear program and the role of key scientists like Dr. R. Chidambaram in establishing India's nuclear deterrence.
Analyze the impact of India's nuclear tests on its foreign policy and security doctrine.
Evaluate the contributions of Indian scientists to national development and strategic autonomy.
Examine the ethical considerations and global implications of nuclear proliferation, with India's stance as a responsible nuclear power.
Trace the journey of India's self-reliance in strategic technologies, highlighting the challenges and successes.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram, a distinguished nuclear scientist.
articles.trivia: Dr. Chidambaram was one of the few scientists to have been associated with both Pokhran-I (1974) and Pokhran-II (1998) nuclear tests.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Governor's Discretionary Powers in Bill Assent
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has decided to hear a batch of petitions challenging the discretionary powers of state Governors regarding giving assent to bills passed by state legislatures.
• The petitions argue that undue delays or withholding of assent by Governors undermine parliamentary democracy and federal principles.
• Concerns have been raised over the 'pocket veto' effectively exercised by some Governors, leading to legislative stalemates.
• The court's decision is expected to clarify the constitutional limits and responsibilities of the Governor's office concerning legislative functions.
• This issue has been a recurring point of contention between the Union and State governments, impacting federal relations.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court to examine Governor's powers regarding bill assent.
Petitions highlight delays/withholding of assent, impacting federalism.
Concerns over 'pocket veto' by Governors.
Article 200 of the Constitution deals with Governor's assent to bills.
Issue frequently arises between state governments and the Governor.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the role of the Governor in the Indian federal structure, particularly concerning legislative powers.
Discuss the constitutional provisions governing the Governor's assent to bills and the controversies surrounding their interpretation.
Evaluate whether the Governor's discretionary powers are a necessary check or an impediment to state autonomy.
Suggest reforms to ensure harmonious functioning between the Governor's office and state legislatures.
Examine the implications of judicial pronouncements on federal relations and the balance of power.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building, symbolizing judicial review.
articles.trivia: The Sarkaria Commission (1983) and the Punchhi Commission (2007) have both recommended reforms regarding the Governor's role and powers.

articles.headline: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee Holds Repo Rate, Cautions on Inflationary Risks
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today announced its decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The MPC cited persistent inflationary pressures, particularly from food prices and global crude oil volatility, as key reasons for maintaining a cautious stance.
• While economic growth remains robust, the RBI emphasized the need to bring inflation within the target band sustainably.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the 4% target.
• Experts anticipate continued vigilance from the RBI, with potential rate cuts unlikely in the immediate future until inflation cools significantly.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Inflationary pressures, especially food and crude oil, cited.
RBI's inflation target is 4% (with a +/- 2% band).
Stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting economic growth in the current global and domestic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in controlling inflation in the Indian context.
Evaluate the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its implications for credit availability and investment.
Examine the interplay between monetary policy and fiscal policy in achieving macroeconomic stability.
Critically assess the factors contributing to food inflation in India and potential policy interventions.
articles.imageDescription: Graph illustrating the trend of the RBI repo rate over time, with a focus on recent stability.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external experts appointed by the government.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Enhanced Maritime Security and Trade Ties
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded today, reaffirming commitment to strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
• Leaders emphasized cooperation in maritime security, counter-terrorism, and disaster management amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics in the Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions also centered on boosting trade and investment, with a focus on reviewing the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) agreement to make it more business-friendly.
• India highlighted its 'Act East' policy, underscoring the importance of ASEAN as a central pillar for regional stability and economic prosperity.
• Joint statements included commitments to address climate change, promote sustainable development, and enhance digital connectivity.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-ASEAN Summit focused on maritime security, counter-terrorism, trade.
Commitment to Comprehensive Strategic Partnership reaffirmed.
Review of ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) discussed.
India's 'Act East' policy is central to its engagement with ASEAN.
ASEAN comprises 10 Southeast Asian nations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of ASEAN for India's 'Act East' policy and its broader Indo-Pacific strategy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing trade and investment ties between India and ASEAN.
Evaluate India's role in promoting maritime security and stability in the Indo-Pacific region in collaboration with ASEAN.
Examine the potential of India-ASEAN cooperation in addressing common regional challenges like climate change and disaster management.
Critically assess the progress and future prospects of the India-ASEAN Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
articles.imageDescription: Photo of leaders from India and ASEAN nations shaking hands during the summit.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was founded in 1967 with five member states: Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand. It now has ten members.

articles.headline: Government Notifies New Coastal Zone Management Plan for Sustainable Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has notified a new Coastal Zone Management Plan (CZMP) aimed at promoting sustainable development while protecting fragile coastal ecosystems.
• The revised plan incorporates lessons from recent extreme weather events and emphasizes climate change adaptation and mitigation strategies for coastal areas.
• It mandates stricter regulations for construction and industrial activities in ecologically sensitive zones, while also facilitating sustainable livelihoods for coastal communities.
• The plan provides for demarcation of hazard lines, vulnerability mapping, and integrated planning for coastal infrastructure.
• States and Union Territories are now tasked with updating their respective CZMPs based on the new national guidelines.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: New Coastal Zone Management Plan (CZMP) notified by MoEFCC.
Aims for sustainable development and coastal ecosystem protection.
Includes climate change adaptation and mitigation strategies.
Mandates stricter regulations for sensitive zones.
States/UTs to update their CZMPs based on new guidelines.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a robust Coastal Zone Management Plan for India's long coastline, considering ecological fragility and livelihood dependency.
Analyze the challenges in implementing CZMPs, including balancing environmental protection with developmental needs and local community concerns.
Evaluate how the new CZMP addresses climate change impacts and disaster preparedness in coastal regions.
Examine the role of technology and community participation in effective coastal zone management.
Critically assess the 'Blue Economy' concept in the context of sustainable coastal development.
articles.imageDescription: Satellite image showing India's coastline with demarcated ecological zones.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km, supporting a significant portion of its population and diverse ecosystems.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-07) aboard the PSLV-C59 rocket from Sriharikota.
• EOS-07 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including urban planning, disaster management, agriculture, and forestry.
• This mission further strengthens India's indigenous capabilities in remote sensing and space-based data collection.
• The satellite carries advanced optical payloads capable of capturing detailed multi-spectral images of Earth's surface.
• The launch also placed several co-passenger satellites for international customers, showcasing India's growing role in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched EOS-07 using PSLV-C59 rocket.
EOS-07 is an Earth Observation Satellite.
Provides high-resolution imagery for urban planning, disaster management, agriculture.
Strengthens India's remote sensing capabilities.
PSLV is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's developmental needs, citing examples in various sectors.
Analyze ISRO's contributions to India's space program and its role in promoting self-reliance in space technology.
Evaluate the economic and strategic implications of India's growing capabilities in remote sensing and geospatial data.
Examine the challenges and future prospects for India's space sector, including private sector participation.
Illustrate how space technology can be leveraged for effective disaster management and climate change monitoring.
articles.imageDescription: Rocket launching into space with the ISRO logo visible, symbolizing a successful mission.
articles.trivia: PSLV is known for its versatility and reliability, often referred to as ISRO's 'workhorse' launch vehicle.

articles.headline: Border Security Forces Enhance Surveillance Along Western Frontier with Advanced Systems
articles.explainer:
• In a move to bolster national security, India's Border Security Forces (BSF) have deployed advanced surveillance systems along the western frontier, particularly in sensitive areas.
• These new systems include integrated drone technology, ground sensors, night vision devices, and artificial intelligence-powered analytics for real-time threat detection.
• The enhancement aims to curb cross-border infiltration, smuggling of arms and narcotics, and other illicit activities more effectively.
• This initiative is part of a broader strategy to modernize border management and integrate technology for comprehensive security coverage.
• Training programs for personnel are also being intensified to ensure optimal utilization of the new sophisticated equipment.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: BSF deployed advanced surveillance systems on western frontier.
Systems include drones, ground sensors, night vision, AI analytics.
Aims to curb infiltration, smuggling, and illicit activities.
Part of broader border management modernization strategy.
Intensified training for personnel on new equipment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of border security challenges in India, particularly along the western frontier.
Analyze the role of technology, such as drones and AI, in enhancing border surveillance and management.
Evaluate the effectiveness of integrated border management systems (IBMS) in preventing cross-border threats.
Examine the challenges faced by border guarding forces and suggest measures for their modernization and capacity building.
Critically assess the balance between security imperatives and human rights concerns in border regions.
articles.imageDescription: A border post at night, illuminated by surveillance lights, with a drone visible in the sky.
articles.trivia: The Border Security Force (BSF) is one of India's seven Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) and guards India's land borders during peacetime.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Significant Milestones in Digital Health Adoption
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has announced significant progress in its mission to create a national digital health ecosystem, with millions of Health ID (ABHA) creations.
• Key milestones include a substantial increase in health facility registrations and digital health records linked to ABHA IDs.
• The mission aims to standardize digital health records, facilitate seamless access to healthcare services, and improve data-driven policymaking.
• Interoperability across various digital health applications and platforms is a core focus, enabling better coordination among healthcare providers.
• The success of ABDM is seen as crucial for achieving universal health coverage and transforming India's healthcare delivery system.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create a national digital health ecosystem.
Milestones include millions of Health ID (ABHA) creations.
Focus on standardizing digital health records.
Interoperability across digital health platforms is a core feature.
Crucial for universal health coverage and healthcare transformation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission to revolutionize healthcare delivery and access in India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a large-scale digital health mission, including data privacy, digital literacy, and infrastructure gaps.
Evaluate how ABDM can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and improving health outcomes.
Examine the role of technology in making healthcare more accessible, affordable, and efficient in India.
Critically assess the ethical considerations involved in collecting and managing sensitive health data in a digital ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating the flow of digital health records and ABHA IDs within the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat program includes two major pillars: Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) and Health and Wellness Centres (HWCs).

articles.headline: India's Manufacturing PMI Sustains Strong Growth in December, Signals Economic Resilience
articles.explainer:
• India's Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) for December remained robust, indicating continued expansion in the sector for the 18th consecutive month.
• The index, compiled by S&P Global, highlighted strong new orders, increased output, and job creation across manufacturing firms.
• This sustained growth is a positive sign for India's economic resilience amidst global headwinds and contributes to overall GDP growth.
• Input costs showed some moderation, but firms continued to pass on higher charges to customers, indicating persistent pricing power.
• The optimistic outlook for future production remains high, driven by strong domestic demand and improving export prospects.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Industrial policy
prelimsPoints: India's Manufacturing PMI for December showed sustained growth.
Indicates expansion for 18th consecutive month.
Highlights strong new orders, output, and job creation.
Compiled by S&P Global.
Positive sign for economic resilience and GDP growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key factors contributing to the sustained growth in India's manufacturing sector as indicated by the PMI.
Discuss the significance of the manufacturing sector for India's economic growth and employment generation.
Evaluate the government's initiatives (e.g., PLI scheme, Make in India) in boosting domestic manufacturing and attracting investment.
Examine the challenges faced by the Indian manufacturing sector, including global supply chain disruptions and competition.
Critically assess the role of various economic indicators like PMI in forecasting economic trends and informing policymaking.
articles.imageDescription: Bar graph showing the trend of India's Manufacturing PMI over the past year, indicating consistent growth.
articles.trivia: A PMI reading above 50 indicates expansion, while a reading below 50 indicates contraction.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Global Climate Finance Initiatives at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India today reiterated its commitment to global climate action by pledging enhanced support for climate finance initiatives at a key United Nations forum.
• The announcement underscores India's dual role as a developing nation requiring climate finance and a responsible global actor contributing to climate solutions.
• India called for developed nations to fulfill their commitment of $100 billion per year in climate finance to developing countries, emphasizing equity and common but differentiated responsibilities.
• Discussions included innovative financing mechanisms, technology transfer, and capacity building to help vulnerable nations adapt to climate change.
• India highlighted its domestic efforts in renewable energy deployment and sustainable development practices as examples of its commitment.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India pledged support for global climate finance initiatives at a UN forum.
Called on developed nations to meet $100 billion climate finance commitment.
Emphasized equity and CBDR principle.
Discussions on innovative financing, technology transfer, capacity building.
India highlighted its domestic renewable energy efforts.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's evolving stance on climate change and climate finance at international forums, balancing its developmental needs with global responsibilities.
Analyze the concept of 'common but differentiated responsibilities' in the context of climate justice and its implications for global climate negotiations.
Evaluate the challenges in mobilizing and disbursing climate finance, particularly for developing countries.
Examine the role of international cooperation and technology transfer in helping vulnerable nations combat climate change.
Critically assess the progress towards achieving the $100 billion climate finance goal and its impact on global climate action.
articles.imageDescription: Delegates from various nations participating in a UN climate conference, with India's flag visible.
articles.trivia: The $100 billion climate finance goal was first agreed upon at the COP15 Copenhagen Summit in 2009.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Electoral Bond Scheme for Greater Transparency
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee on Law and Justice has submitted a report recommending significant reforms to the Electoral Bond Scheme to ensure greater transparency in political funding.
• The committee suggested disclosing the identity of donors and donee political parties to the Election Commission of India (ECI), a departure from the current anonymity feature.
• Concerns have been raised by civil society groups and opposition parties about the opacity of electoral bonds and their potential for quid pro quo arrangements.
• The recommendations aim to address these concerns by enhancing accountability and preventing misuse of the funding mechanism.
• The report calls for a broader debate on electoral reforms to strengthen democratic processes in India.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary panel recommended reforms for Electoral Bond Scheme.
Suggested disclosing donor and donee identities to ECI.
Current scheme ensures anonymity of donors.
Aims to enhance transparency and accountability.
Electoral bonds introduced in 2018.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Electoral Bond Scheme in India, highlighting its objectives and the controversies surrounding its implementation.
Discuss the arguments for and against the anonymity of donors in political funding.
Evaluate the recommendations of the parliamentary committee and their potential impact on transparency and electoral integrity.
Examine the broader challenges of political funding in India and suggest comprehensive electoral reforms.
Assess the role of the Election Commission of India and the judiciary in ensuring fairness and transparency in elections.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing the flow of funds through electoral bonds, with a question mark over transparency.
articles.trivia: The Electoral Bond Scheme allows individuals and corporate entities to donate money to political parties anonymously.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Initiatives for Urban Air Quality Improvement in Major Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today unveiled a new set of initiatives under the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) to significantly improve urban air quality in major Indian cities.
• These initiatives focus on source apportionment studies, real-time monitoring networks, and targeted interventions for industrial, vehicular, and construction dust pollution.
• Special emphasis will be placed on promoting electric vehicles, enhancing public transport infrastructure, and adopting cleaner industrial technologies.
• The plan involves greater coordination between central and state agencies, along with public awareness campaigns for behavioral change.
• Funding mechanisms and performance-based incentives for cities achieving air quality targets have also been outlined.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: New initiatives launched under National Clean Air Programme (NCAP).
Focus on source apportionment, real-time monitoring, targeted interventions.
Emphasis on EVs, public transport, cleaner industrial tech.
Involves central-state coordination and public awareness.
NCAP aims to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024 (initial target).
mainsPoints: Discuss the major sources of urban air pollution in India and their adverse impacts on public health and the environment.
Analyze the effectiveness of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) in addressing air pollution challenges.
Evaluate the role of technological solutions and policy interventions in improving urban air quality.
Examine the challenges in implementing air pollution control measures, including inter-state coordination and public cooperation.
Suggest a multi-pronged strategy for sustainable urban air quality management in Indian cities.
articles.imageDescription: Image of a city skyline shrouded in smog, with an overlay of clean air symbols.
articles.trivia: India is home to many of the world's most polluted cities, making air quality improvement a critical national priority.

articles.headline: Breakthrough: Indigenous Vaccine for Neglected Tropical Disease Shows Promising Results
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists have announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous vaccine for a neglected tropical disease (NTD), showing promising results in clinical trials.
• The vaccine, developed by a collaborative effort of ICMR and a private pharmaceutical company, targets a specific NTD prevalent in several parts of India and other developing nations.
• Neglected Tropical Diseases affect over a billion people globally, primarily in low-income populations, causing debilitating health issues.
• This development underscores India's growing capabilities in vaccine research and development, contributing to global health security.
• The successful trial paves the way for accelerated regulatory approval and eventual mass production, potentially offering an affordable solution.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Developments in Science and Technology and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Indigenous vaccine developed for a Neglected Tropical Disease (NTD).
Collaborative effort between ICMR and private firm.
NTDs affect over a billion people, primarily in low-income regions.
Highlights India's vaccine R&D capabilities.
Aims for accelerated regulatory approval and mass production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the burden of Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in India and their socio-economic impact on vulnerable populations.
Analyze India's role in global vaccine development and its potential to provide affordable healthcare solutions to developing countries.
Evaluate the challenges in developing and deploying vaccines for NTDs, including funding, research infrastructure, and public health awareness.
Examine the importance of public-private partnerships in accelerating medical research and innovation.
Critically assess the government's initiatives to combat NTDs and improve public health infrastructure.
articles.imageDescription: Microscopic view of a vaccine vial, symbolizing scientific breakthrough in medicine.
articles.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) lists 20 diseases as Neglected Tropical Diseases, including Dengue, Chikungunya, and Lymphatic Filariasis.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Initiates Major Restoration Project for Historic Temple Complex
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has commenced a large-scale restoration project for a significant historic temple complex in Southern India, aiming to preserve its architectural grandeur.
• The multi-year project involves meticulous structural conservation, chemical preservation of ancient frescoes, and landscape restoration around the site.
• This initiative is part of ASI's broader mandate to protect and maintain monuments and archaeological sites of national importance.
• Local communities and heritage experts are being consulted to ensure the authenticity and cultural sensitivity of the restoration work.
• The project is expected to boost cultural tourism and enhance public awareness about India's rich architectural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI started major restoration of a historic temple complex in South India.
Project includes structural conservation, chemical preservation, landscape restoration.
Part of ASI's mandate to protect national monuments.
Consulting local communities and heritage experts.
Aims to boost cultural tourism and awareness.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving India's architectural heritage for cultural identity and tourism.
Analyze the role and challenges of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in protecting and maintaining ancient monuments.
Evaluate the methods and ethical considerations involved in large-scale heritage restoration projects.
Examine the balance between conservation efforts and the developmental needs of surrounding areas.
Suggest ways to enhance public participation and awareness in heritage preservation initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: Detailed image of an ancient temple facade undergoing restoration, with scaffolding visible.
articles.trivia: The ASI, established in 1861, is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Vocational Training Program for Rural Youth Empowerment
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship today launched a new vocational training program specifically designed to empower rural youth with market-relevant skills.
• The program focuses on sectors with high employment potential in rural and semi-urban areas, such as agriculture technology, renewable energy installation, digital services, and traditional crafts.
• It aims to reduce rural-urban migration by creating local employment opportunities and fostering entrepreneurship among the youth.
• Partnerships with local industries, NGOs, and skill development centers are central to the program's implementation and job placement efforts.
• The initiative is aligned with the 'Skill India' mission and seeks to bridge the skill gap in emerging rural economies.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
articles.source: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsPoints: New vocational training program launched for rural youth.
Focus on agri-tech, renewable energy, digital services, traditional crafts.
Aims to reduce rural-urban migration and foster entrepreneurship.
Partnerships with local industries and NGOs.
Aligned with 'Skill India' mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by rural youth in accessing quality education and employment opportunities in India.
Analyze the significance of vocational training in harnessing India's demographic dividend and promoting inclusive growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives like 'Skill India' in addressing the skill gap and unemployment.
Examine how localized skill development programs can curb rural-urban migration and strengthen rural economies.
Suggest strategies for integrating traditional crafts and knowledge systems into modern vocational training frameworks.
articles.imageDescription: Group of young rural individuals learning practical skills in a workshop setting.
articles.trivia: India has the world's largest youth population, making skill development crucial for its economic future.

articles.headline: IMF Report Highlights Global Economic Slowdown, Notes India's Relative Resilience
articles.explainer:
• The International Monetary Fund (IMF) today released its latest Global Economic Outlook report, projecting a continued slowdown in global growth for the coming year.
• The report cited persistent inflation, geopolitical tensions, and tighter monetary policies in major economies as key drivers of the deceleration.
• While the global outlook remains challenging, the IMF noted India's relative resilience, projecting it to remain one of the fastest-growing major economies.
• India's strong domestic demand, public investment, and structural reforms were highlighted as factors supporting its growth trajectory.
• The report, however, cautioned India about the risks from global spillovers and the need for continued fiscal prudence.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: IMF projected global economic slowdown.
Cited inflation, geopolitical tensions, tight monetary policies.
Noted India's relative economic resilience.
India's growth supported by domestic demand, public investment, reforms.
IMF cautioned India on global spillovers and fiscal prudence.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the current global economic slowdown and their potential impact on India.
Discuss the reasons behind India's relative economic resilience amidst global headwinds, as noted by the IMF.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in maintaining its growth trajectory in a volatile global economic environment.
Examine the importance of fiscal prudence and structural reforms in ensuring long-term economic stability and growth.
Critically assess the role of international financial institutions like the IMF in global economic governance and surveillance.
articles.imageDescription: World map with economic growth indicators, highlighting India's position.
articles.trivia: The IMF was established in 1944 at the Bretton Woods Conference, along with the World Bank.

highlights: Government launches comprehensive National Digital Public Infrastructure Policy for inclusive growth and efficient service delivery.
Veteran nuclear scientist Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram, a key architect of India's atomic program, passes away at 88.
Supreme Court to examine Governor's discretionary powers regarding assent to state bills, addressing federalism concerns.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee holds repo rate steady, citing persistent inflationary pressures, particularly from food prices.
India-ASEAN Summit concludes with commitments to enhanced maritime security, trade, and review of the AIFTA agreement.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's National Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Policy, consider the following statements:
1. The policy aims to create interoperable digital platforms for identity, payments, and data exchange.
2. It explicitly prohibits public-private partnerships in the development and deployment of DPI solutions.
3. The policy is inspired by the success of initiatives like Aadhaar and UPI.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The DPI policy focuses on building interoperable digital platforms for identity, payments, data exchange, and governance. Statement 2 is incorrect: The policy explicitly seeks to create a robust framework for public-private partnerships in developing and deploying DPI solutions. Statement 3 is correct: The policy is inspired by the success of Aadhaar and UPI.

prelimsMocks.question:
Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram, who recently passed away, was closely associated with which of the following?
(a) India's Space Program (ISRO)
(b) India's Green Revolution
(c) India's Nuclear Program
(d) India's IT Revolution
prelimsMocks.explanation: Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram was a pivotal figure in India's nuclear program, serving as the Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission and playing a crucial role in the Pokhran-II nuclear tests in 1998.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Governor's powers concerning bills passed by the State Legislature in India:
1. The Governor can withhold assent to a bill, which effectively acts as an absolute veto.
2. The Governor can reserve a bill for the consideration of the President.
3. The Constitution specifies a time limit within which the Governor must give assent or reserve a bill.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: While the Governor can withhold assent (Article 200), it's not an 'absolute veto' in the sense that if the bill is passed again by the legislature with or without amendments, the Governor *shall not* withhold assent (though they can still reserve it for the President). The term 'pocket veto' refers to indefinite inaction. Statement 2 is correct: Article 200 allows the Governor to reserve a bill for the consideration of the President. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Constitution does not specify a time limit for the Governor to give assent or reserve a bill, which is a major point of contention.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following bodies is responsible for determining the benchmark interest rates in India, such as the Repo Rate?
(a) Ministry of Finance
(b) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Board of Directors
(c) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
(d) Indian Banks' Association (IBA)
options: (b) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Board of Directors
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body (three from RBI, three external) constituted under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, responsible for determining the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question:
The 'Act East Policy' is a significant foreign policy initiative of India. Which of the following regional groupings is a central pillar of this policy?
(a) SAARC
(b) BIMSTEC
(c) ASEAN
(d) SCO
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Act East Policy' is India's diplomatic initiative to promote economic, strategic, and cultural relations with the Asia-Pacific region at a broader level. ASEAN is considered a central pillar of India's Act East Policy, crucial for regional stability and economic prosperity.

mainsMocks.question: The recent unveiling of the National Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Policy signifies India's commitment to leveraging technology for inclusive development. Discuss the potential benefits and challenges associated with the implementation of such a comprehensive DPI, and suggest measures to ensure its equitable and secure adoption across the nation. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce DPI policy and its aim. List benefits like efficient service delivery, financial inclusion, innovation. Discuss challenges: data privacy, security breaches, digital divide, infrastructure gaps. Suggest measures: robust data protection laws, digital literacy campaigns, universal internet access, public-private collaboration, ethical AI usage.

mainsMocks.question: Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram's demise marks the end of an era for India's strategic autonomy. Analyze the critical role played by scientists like him in the development of India's nuclear program and its implications for national security and international relations. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues. GS3: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Dr. Chidambaram's contributions (AEC, Pokhran-II). Discuss the historical context of India's nuclear program (self-reliance, peaceful uses, deterrence). Highlight how scientists enabled strategic autonomy, defying technology denial regimes. Analyze implications for national security (credible minimum deterrence) and international relations (responsible nuclear power, NPT debate). Conclude on the legacy of scientific leadership.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's decision to hear petitions on the Governor's discretionary powers concerning bill assent brings to the fore persistent issues of federal harmony. Examine the constitutional provisions governing the Governor's legislative role and critically evaluate how delays in bill assent can undermine parliamentary democracy and federal principles in India. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure; Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by stating the constitutional position of the Governor (Article 200, 201). Explain discretionary powers (withholding assent, reserving for President). Discuss how delays or non-assent can impact state governance, legislative agenda, and federal balance. Use terms like 'pocket veto,' 'constitutional morality.' Critically analyze the implications for parliamentary democracy (stalling elected government's will) and federalism (central intervention). Suggest potential reforms or judicial clarity.

quickBytes.text: The National Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Policy aims to integrate various digital platforms for citizen services.
Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram was a key figure in India's Pokhran-II nuclear tests in 1998.
Article 200 of the Indian Constitution deals with the Governor's power to assent to bills.
The Repo Rate is the rate at which the Reserve Bank of India lends money to commercial banks.
India's 'Act East Policy' focuses on strengthening ties with Southeast Asian nations, with ASEAN as a core component.
The Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) rules regulate developmental activities along India's coastline.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's versatile workhorse rocket for launching satellites.
The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) aims to create a national digital health ecosystem using ABHA IDs.
A Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) reading above 50 indicates expansion in the manufacturing sector.
The National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) aims to reduce particulate matter concentration in Indian cities.

GS1.headline: Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram, Nuclear Scientist and Architect of India's Atomic Program, Passes Away at 88
GS1.explainer:
• Renowned Indian nuclear scientist Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram passed away today at the age of 88, leaving behind a profound legacy in India's scientific landscape.
• Dr. Chidambaram was a pivotal figure in India's nuclear program, serving as the Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission (AEC) and Principal Scientific Adviser to the Government of India.
• He played a crucial role in the Pokhran-II nuclear tests in 1998, providing critical scientific leadership and technical expertise.
• His contributions extended to materials science, high-pressure physics, and development of advanced nuclear reactors.
• Dr. Chidambaram was a recipient of the Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian honor, recognizing his immense services to the nation.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
prelimsPoints: Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram (1936-2025) was a leading Indian nuclear scientist.
Served as Chairman of Atomic Energy Commission and Principal Scientific Adviser.
Instrumental in the Pokhran-II nuclear tests (1998).
Awarded Padma Vibhushan.
Contributions in materials science and high-pressure physics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of India's nuclear program and the role of key scientists like Dr. R. Chidambaram in establishing India's nuclear deterrence.
Analyze the impact of India's nuclear tests on its foreign policy and security doctrine.
Evaluate the contributions of Indian scientists to national development and strategic autonomy.
Examine the ethical considerations and global implications of nuclear proliferation, with India's stance as a responsible nuclear power.
Trace the journey of India's self-reliance in strategic technologies, highlighting the challenges and successes.
GS1.imageDescription: Portrait of Dr. Rajagopala Chidambaram, a distinguished nuclear scientist.
GS1.trivia: Dr. Chidambaram was one of the few scientists to have been associated with both Pokhran-I (1974) and Pokhran-II (1998) nuclear tests.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Initiates Major Restoration Project for Historic Temple Complex
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has commenced a large-scale restoration project for a significant historic temple complex in Southern India, aiming to preserve its architectural grandeur.
• The multi-year project involves meticulous structural conservation, chemical preservation of ancient frescoes, and landscape restoration around the site.
• This initiative is part of ASI's broader mandate to protect and maintain monuments and archaeological sites of national importance.
• Local communities and heritage experts are being consulted to ensure the authenticity and cultural sensitivity of the restoration work.
• The project is expected to boost cultural tourism and enhance public awareness about India's rich architectural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI started major restoration of a historic temple complex in South India.
Project includes structural conservation, chemical preservation, landscape restoration.
Part of ASI's mandate to protect national monuments.
Consulting local communities and heritage experts.
Aims to boost cultural tourism and awareness.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving India's architectural heritage for cultural identity and tourism.
Analyze the role and challenges of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in protecting and maintaining ancient monuments.
Evaluate the methods and ethical considerations involved in large-scale heritage restoration projects.
Examine the balance between conservation efforts and the developmental needs of surrounding areas.
Suggest ways to enhance public participation and awareness in heritage preservation initiatives.
GS1.imageDescription: Detailed image of an ancient temple facade undergoing restoration, with scaffolding visible.
GS1.trivia: The ASI, established in 1861, is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.

GS2.headline: Government Unveils Ambitious National Digital Public Infrastructure Policy
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government today announced a comprehensive National Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Policy, aimed at leveraging technology for inclusive growth and efficient service delivery.
• The policy focuses on building interoperable digital platforms for identity, payments, data exchange, and governance, inspired by the success of Aadhaar and UPI.
• Key objectives include enhancing digital literacy, fostering innovation in the tech ecosystem, and ensuring data privacy and security for all citizens.
• It seeks to create a robust framework for public-private partnerships in developing and deploying DPI solutions across various sectors.
• The policy is expected to streamline access to government services, boost digital economy, and bridge the digital divide, especially in rural areas.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Role of IT in governance
prelimsPoints: National Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Policy aims for inclusive growth and service delivery.
Focus areas: identity, payments, data exchange, governance platforms.
Inspired by Aadhaar and UPI models.
Key objectives: digital literacy, innovation, data privacy, security.
Promotes public-private partnerships in tech development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of DPI to transform governance and public service delivery in India, citing examples like Aadhaar and UPI.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a national DPI policy, including data security, privacy concerns, and digital exclusion.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships in building and scaling digital infrastructure.
Examine how DPI can contribute to India's economic growth and social equity.
Critically assess the 'Digital India' vision in light of this new policy.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration depicting interconnected digital nodes and services, symbolizing Digital Public Infrastructure.
GS2.trivia: India's UPI system processes more transactions than many global payment systems combined, showcasing the power of effective DPI.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Governor's Discretionary Powers in Bill Assent
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has decided to hear a batch of petitions challenging the discretionary powers of state Governors regarding giving assent to bills passed by state legislatures.
• The petitions argue that undue delays or withholding of assent by Governors undermine parliamentary democracy and federal principles.
• Concerns have been raised over the 'pocket veto' effectively exercised by some Governors, leading to legislative stalemates.
• The court's decision is expected to clarify the constitutional limits and responsibilities of the Governor's office concerning legislative functions.
• This issue has been a recurring point of contention between the Union and State governments, impacting federal relations.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court to examine Governor's powers regarding bill assent.
Petitions highlight delays/withholding of assent, impacting federalism.
Concerns over 'pocket veto' by Governors.
Article 200 of the Constitution deals with Governor's assent to bills.
Issue frequently arises between state governments and the Governor.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the role of the Governor in the Indian federal structure, particularly concerning legislative powers.
Discuss the constitutional provisions governing the Governor's assent to bills and the controversies surrounding their interpretation.
Evaluate whether the Governor's discretionary powers are a necessary check or an impediment to state autonomy.
Suggest reforms to ensure harmonious functioning between the Governor's office and state legislatures.
Examine the implications of judicial pronouncements on federal relations and the balance of power.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building, symbolizing judicial review.
GS2.trivia: The Sarkaria Commission (1983) and the Punchhi Commission (2007) have both recommended reforms regarding the Governor's role and powers.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Enhanced Maritime Security and Trade Ties
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded today, reaffirming commitment to strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
• Leaders emphasized cooperation in maritime security, counter-terrorism, and disaster management amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics in the Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions also centered on boosting trade and investment, with a focus on reviewing the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) agreement to make it more business-friendly.
• India highlighted its 'Act East' policy, underscoring the importance of ASEAN as a central pillar for regional stability and economic prosperity.
• Joint statements included commitments to address climate change, promote sustainable development, and enhance digital connectivity.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-ASEAN Summit focused on maritime security, counter-terrorism, trade.
Commitment to Comprehensive Strategic Partnership reaffirmed.
Review of ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) discussed.
India's 'Act East' policy is central to its engagement with ASEAN.
ASEAN comprises 10 Southeast Asian nations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of ASEAN for India's 'Act East' policy and its broader Indo-Pacific strategy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing trade and investment ties between India and ASEAN.
Evaluate India's role in promoting maritime security and stability in the Indo-Pacific region in collaboration with ASEAN.
Examine the potential of India-ASEAN cooperation in addressing common regional challenges like climate change and disaster management.
Critically assess the progress and future prospects of the India-ASEAN Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
GS2.imageDescription: Photo of leaders from India and ASEAN nations shaking hands during the summit.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was founded in 1967 with five member states: Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand. It now has ten members.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Significant Milestones in Digital Health Adoption
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has announced significant progress in its mission to create a national digital health ecosystem, with millions of Health ID (ABHA) creations.
• Key milestones include a substantial increase in health facility registrations and digital health records linked to ABHA IDs.
• The mission aims to standardize digital health records, facilitate seamless access to healthcare services, and improve data-driven policymaking.
• Interoperability across various digital health applications and platforms is a core focus, enabling better coordination among healthcare providers.
• The success of ABDM is seen as crucial for achieving universal health coverage and transforming India's healthcare delivery system.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create a national digital health ecosystem.
Milestones include millions of Health ID (ABHA) creations.
Focus on standardizing digital health records.
Interoperability across digital health platforms is a core feature.
Crucial for universal health coverage and healthcare transformation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission to revolutionize healthcare delivery and access in India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a large-scale digital health mission, including data privacy, digital literacy, and infrastructure gaps.
Evaluate how ABDM can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and improving health outcomes.
Examine the role of technology in making healthcare more accessible, affordable, and efficient in India.
Critically assess the ethical considerations involved in collecting and managing sensitive health data in a digital ecosystem.
GS2.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating the flow of digital health records and ABHA IDs within the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat program includes two major pillars: Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) and Health and Wellness Centres (HWCs).

GS2.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Global Climate Finance Initiatives at UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India today reiterated its commitment to global climate action by pledging enhanced support for climate finance initiatives at a key United Nations forum.
• The announcement underscores India's dual role as a developing nation requiring climate finance and a responsible global actor contributing to climate solutions.
• India called for developed nations to fulfill their commitment of $100 billion per year in climate finance to developing countries, emphasizing equity and common but differentiated responsibilities.
• Discussions included innovative financing mechanisms, technology transfer, and capacity building to help vulnerable nations adapt to climate change.
• India highlighted its domestic efforts in renewable energy deployment and sustainable development practices as examples of its commitment.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India pledged support for global climate finance initiatives at a UN forum.
Called on developed nations to meet $100 billion climate finance commitment.
Emphasized equity and CBDR principle.
Discussions on innovative financing, technology transfer, capacity building.
India highlighted its domestic renewable energy efforts.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's evolving stance on climate change and climate finance at international forums, balancing its developmental needs with global responsibilities.
Analyze the concept of 'common but differentiated responsibilities' in the context of climate justice and its implications for global climate negotiations.
Evaluate the challenges in mobilizing and disbursing climate finance, particularly for developing countries.
Examine the role of international cooperation and technology transfer in helping vulnerable nations combat climate change.
Critically assess the progress towards achieving the $100 billion climate finance goal and its impact on global climate action.
GS2.imageDescription: Delegates from various nations participating in a UN climate conference, with India's flag visible.
GS2.trivia: The $100 billion climate finance goal was first agreed upon at the COP15 Copenhagen Summit in 2009.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Electoral Bond Scheme for Greater Transparency
GS2.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee on Law and Justice has submitted a report recommending significant reforms to the Electoral Bond Scheme to ensure greater transparency in political funding.
• The committee suggested disclosing the identity of donors and donee political parties to the Election Commission of India (ECI), a departure from the current anonymity feature.
• Concerns have been raised by civil society groups and opposition parties about the opacity of electoral bonds and their potential for quid pro quo arrangements.
• The recommendations aim to address these concerns by enhancing accountability and preventing misuse of the funding mechanism.
• The report calls for a broader debate on electoral reforms to strengthen democratic processes in India.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary panel recommended reforms for Electoral Bond Scheme.
Suggested disclosing donor and donee identities to ECI.
Current scheme ensures anonymity of donors.
Aims to enhance transparency and accountability.
Electoral bonds introduced in 2018.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Electoral Bond Scheme in India, highlighting its objectives and the controversies surrounding its implementation.
Discuss the arguments for and against the anonymity of donors in political funding.
Evaluate the recommendations of the parliamentary committee and their potential impact on transparency and electoral integrity.
Examine the broader challenges of political funding in India and suggest comprehensive electoral reforms.
Assess the role of the Election Commission of India and the judiciary in ensuring fairness and transparency in elections.
GS2.imageDescription: Graphic showing the flow of funds through electoral bonds, with a question mark over transparency.
GS2.trivia: The Electoral Bond Scheme allows individuals and corporate entities to donate money to political parties anonymously.

GS2.headline: Government Launches New Vocational Training Program for Rural Youth Empowerment
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship today launched a new vocational training program specifically designed to empower rural youth with market-relevant skills.
• The program focuses on sectors with high employment potential in rural and semi-urban areas, such as agriculture technology, renewable energy installation, digital services, and traditional crafts.
• It aims to reduce rural-urban migration by creating local employment opportunities and fostering entrepreneurship among the youth.
• Partnerships with local industries, NGOs, and skill development centers are central to the program's implementation and job placement efforts.
• The initiative is aligned with the 'Skill India' mission and seeks to bridge the skill gap in emerging rural economies.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
GS2.source: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsPoints: New vocational training program launched for rural youth.
Focus on agri-tech, renewable energy, digital services, traditional crafts.
Aims to reduce rural-urban migration and foster entrepreneurship.
Partnerships with local industries and NGOs.
Aligned with 'Skill India' mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by rural youth in accessing quality education and employment opportunities in India.
Analyze the significance of vocational training in harnessing India's demographic dividend and promoting inclusive growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives like 'Skill India' in addressing the skill gap and unemployment.
Examine how localized skill development programs can curb rural-urban migration and strengthen rural economies.
Suggest strategies for integrating traditional crafts and knowledge systems into modern vocational training frameworks.
GS2.imageDescription: Group of young rural individuals learning practical skills in a workshop setting.
GS2.trivia: India has the world's largest youth population, making skill development crucial for its economic future.

GS3.headline: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee Holds Repo Rate, Cautions on Inflationary Risks
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today announced its decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The MPC cited persistent inflationary pressures, particularly from food prices and global crude oil volatility, as key reasons for maintaining a cautious stance.
• While economic growth remains robust, the RBI emphasized the need to bring inflation within the target band sustainably.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the 4% target.
• Experts anticipate continued vigilance from the RBI, with potential rate cuts unlikely in the immediate future until inflation cools significantly.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Inflationary pressures, especially food and crude oil, cited.
RBI's inflation target is 4% (with a +/- 2% band).
Stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting economic growth in the current global and domestic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in controlling inflation in the Indian context.
Evaluate the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its implications for credit availability and investment.
Examine the interplay between monetary policy and fiscal policy in achieving macroeconomic stability.
Critically assess the factors contributing to food inflation in India and potential policy interventions.
GS3.imageDescription: Graph illustrating the trend of the RBI repo rate over time, with a focus on recent stability.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external experts appointed by the government.

GS3.headline: Government Notifies New Coastal Zone Management Plan for Sustainable Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has notified a new Coastal Zone Management Plan (CZMP) aimed at promoting sustainable development while protecting fragile coastal ecosystems.
• The revised plan incorporates lessons from recent extreme weather events and emphasizes climate change adaptation and mitigation strategies for coastal areas.
• It mandates stricter regulations for construction and industrial activities in ecologically sensitive zones, while also facilitating sustainable livelihoods for coastal communities.
• The plan provides for demarcation of hazard lines, vulnerability mapping, and integrated planning for coastal infrastructure.
• States and Union Territories are now tasked with updating their respective CZMPs based on the new national guidelines.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: New Coastal Zone Management Plan (CZMP) notified by MoEFCC.
Aims for sustainable development and coastal ecosystem protection.
Includes climate change adaptation and mitigation strategies.
Mandates stricter regulations for sensitive zones.
States/UTs to update their CZMPs based on new guidelines.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a robust Coastal Zone Management Plan for India's long coastline, considering ecological fragility and livelihood dependency.
Analyze the challenges in implementing CZMPs, including balancing environmental protection with developmental needs and local community concerns.
Evaluate how the new CZMP addresses climate change impacts and disaster preparedness in coastal regions.
Examine the role of technology and community participation in effective coastal zone management.
Critically assess the 'Blue Economy' concept in the context of sustainable coastal development.
GS3.imageDescription: Satellite image showing India's coastline with demarcated ecological zones.
GS3.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km, supporting a significant portion of its population and diverse ecosystems.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-07) aboard the PSLV-C59 rocket from Sriharikota.
• EOS-07 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including urban planning, disaster management, agriculture, and forestry.
• This mission further strengthens India's indigenous capabilities in remote sensing and space-based data collection.
• The satellite carries advanced optical payloads capable of capturing detailed multi-spectral images of Earth's surface.
• The launch also placed several co-passenger satellites for international customers, showcasing India's growing role in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched EOS-07 using PSLV-C59 rocket.
EOS-07 is an Earth Observation Satellite.
Provides high-resolution imagery for urban planning, disaster management, agriculture.
Strengthens India's remote sensing capabilities.
PSLV is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's developmental needs, citing examples in various sectors.
Analyze ISRO's contributions to India's space program and its role in promoting self-reliance in space technology.
Evaluate the economic and strategic implications of India's growing capabilities in remote sensing and geospatial data.
Examine the challenges and future prospects for India's space sector, including private sector participation.
Illustrate how space technology can be leveraged for effective disaster management and climate change monitoring.
GS3.imageDescription: Rocket launching into space with the ISRO logo visible, symbolizing a successful mission.
GS3.trivia: PSLV is known for its versatility and reliability, often referred to as ISRO's 'workhorse' launch vehicle.

GS3.headline: Border Security Forces Enhance Surveillance Along Western Frontier with Advanced Systems
GS3.explainer:
• In a move to bolster national security, India's Border Security Forces (BSF) have deployed advanced surveillance systems along the western frontier, particularly in sensitive areas.
• These new systems include integrated drone technology, ground sensors, night vision devices, and artificial intelligence-powered analytics for real-time threat detection.
• The enhancement aims to curb cross-border infiltration, smuggling of arms and narcotics, and other illicit activities more effectively.
• This initiative is part of a broader strategy to modernize border management and integrate technology for comprehensive security coverage.
• Training programs for personnel are also being intensified to ensure optimal utilization of the new sophisticated equipment.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: BSF deployed advanced surveillance systems on western frontier.
Systems include drones, ground sensors, night vision, AI analytics.
Aims to curb infiltration, smuggling, and illicit activities.
Part of broader border management modernization strategy.
Intensified training for personnel on new equipment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of border security challenges in India, particularly along the western frontier.
Analyze the role of technology, such as drones and AI, in enhancing border surveillance and management.
Evaluate the effectiveness of integrated border management systems (IBMS) in preventing cross-border threats.
Examine the challenges faced by border guarding forces and suggest measures for their modernization and capacity building.
Critically assess the balance between security imperatives and human rights concerns in border regions.
GS3.imageDescription: A border post at night, illuminated by surveillance lights, with a drone visible in the sky.
GS3.trivia: The Border Security Force (BSF) is one of India's seven Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) and guards India's land borders during peacetime.

GS3.headline: India's Manufacturing PMI Sustains Strong Growth in December, Signals Economic Resilience
GS3.explainer:
• India's Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) for December remained robust, indicating continued expansion in the sector for the 18th consecutive month.
• The index, compiled by S&P Global, highlighted strong new orders, increased output, and job creation across manufacturing firms.
• This sustained growth is a positive sign for India's economic resilience amidst global headwinds and contributes to overall GDP growth.
• Input costs showed some moderation, but firms continued to pass on higher charges to customers, indicating persistent pricing power.
• The optimistic outlook for future production remains high, driven by strong domestic demand and improving export prospects.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Industrial policy
prelimsPoints: India's Manufacturing PMI for December showed sustained growth.
Indicates expansion for 18th consecutive month.
Highlights strong new orders, output, and job creation.
Compiled by S&P Global.
Positive sign for economic resilience and GDP growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key factors contributing to the sustained growth in India's manufacturing sector as indicated by the PMI.
Discuss the significance of the manufacturing sector for India's economic growth and employment generation.
Evaluate the government's initiatives (e.g., PLI scheme, Make in India) in boosting domestic manufacturing and attracting investment.
Examine the challenges faced by the Indian manufacturing sector, including global supply chain disruptions and competition.
Critically assess the role of various economic indicators like PMI in forecasting economic trends and informing policymaking.
GS3.imageDescription: Bar graph showing the trend of India's Manufacturing PMI over the past year, indicating consistent growth.
GS3.trivia: A PMI reading above 50 indicates expansion, while a reading below 50 indicates contraction.

GS3.headline: Government Launches New Initiatives for Urban Air Quality Improvement in Major Cities
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today unveiled a new set of initiatives under the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) to significantly improve urban air quality in major Indian cities.
• These initiatives focus on source apportionment studies, real-time monitoring networks, and targeted interventions for industrial, vehicular, and construction dust pollution.
• Special emphasis will be placed on promoting electric vehicles, enhancing public transport infrastructure, and adopting cleaner industrial technologies.
• The plan involves greater coordination between central and state agencies, along with public awareness campaigns for behavioral change.
• Funding mechanisms and performance-based incentives for cities achieving air quality targets have also been outlined.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: New initiatives launched under National Clean Air Programme (NCAP).
Focus on source apportionment, real-time monitoring, targeted interventions.
Emphasis on EVs, public transport, cleaner industrial tech.
Involves central-state coordination and public awareness.
NCAP aims to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024 (initial target).
mainsPoints: Discuss the major sources of urban air pollution in India and their adverse impacts on public health and the environment.
Analyze the effectiveness of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) in addressing air pollution challenges.
Evaluate the role of technological solutions and policy interventions in improving urban air quality.
Examine the challenges in implementing air pollution control measures, including inter-state coordination and public cooperation.
Suggest a multi-pronged strategy for sustainable urban air quality management in Indian cities.
GS3.imageDescription: Image of a city skyline shrouded in smog, with an overlay of clean air symbols.
GS3.trivia: India is home to many of the world's most polluted cities, making air quality improvement a critical national priority.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough: Indigenous Vaccine for Neglected Tropical Disease Shows Promising Results
GS3.explainer:
