• Indian scientists have announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous vaccine for a neglected tropical disease (NTD), showing promising results in clinical trials.
• The vaccine, developed by a collaborative effort of ICMR and a private pharmaceutical company, targets a specific NTD prevalent in several parts of India and other developing nations.
• Neglected Tropical Diseases affect over a billion people globally, primarily in low-income populations, causing debilitating health issues.
• This development underscores India's growing capabilities in vaccine research and development, contributing to global health security.
• The successful trial paves the way for accelerated regulatory approval and eventual mass production, potentially offering an affordable solution.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Developments in Science and Technology and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Indigenous vaccine developed for a Neglected Tropical Disease (NTD).
Collaborative effort between ICMR and private firm.
NTDs affect over a billion people, primarily in low-income regions.
Highlights India's vaccine R&D capabilities.
Aims for accelerated regulatory approval and mass production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the burden of Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in India and their socio-economic impact on vulnerable populations.
Analyze India's role in global vaccine development and its potential to provide affordable healthcare solutions to developing countries.
Evaluate the challenges in developing and deploying vaccines for NTDs, including funding, research infrastructure, and public health awareness.
Examine the importance of public-private partnerships in accelerating medical research and innovation.
Critically assess the government's initiatives to combat NTDs and improve public health infrastructure.
GS3.imageDescription: Microscopic view of a vaccine vial, symbolizing scientific breakthrough in medicine.
GS3.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) lists 20 diseases as Neglected Tropical Diseases, including Dengue, Chikungunya, and Lymphatic Filariasis.

GS3.headline: IMF Report Highlights Global Economic Slowdown, Notes India's Relative Resilience
GS3.explainer:
• The International Monetary Fund (IMF) today released its latest Global Economic Outlook report, projecting a continued slowdown in global growth for the coming year.
• The report cited persistent inflation, geopolitical tensions, and tighter monetary policies in major economies as key drivers of the deceleration.
• While the global outlook remains challenging, the IMF noted India's relative resilience, projecting it to remain one of the fastest-growing major economies.
• India's strong domestic demand, public investment, and structural reforms were highlighted as factors supporting its growth trajectory.
• The report, however, cautioned India about the risks from global spillovers and the need for continued fiscal prudence.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: IMF projected global economic slowdown.
Cited inflation, geopolitical tensions, tight monetary policies.
Noted India's relative economic resilience.
India's growth supported by domestic demand, public investment, reforms.
IMF cautioned India on global spillovers and fiscal prudence.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the current global economic slowdown and their potential impact on India.
Discuss the reasons behind India's relative economic resilience amidst global headwinds, as noted by the IMF.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in maintaining its growth trajectory in a volatile global economic environment.
Examine the importance of fiscal prudence and structural reforms in ensuring long-term economic stability and growth.
Critically assess the role of international financial institutions like the IMF in global economic governance and surveillance.
GS3.imageDescription: World map with economic growth indicators, highlighting India's position.
GS3.trivia: The IMF was established in 1944 at the Bretton Woods Conference, along with the World Bank.

sources: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
